acceptance_targets: [ch08_craft_001_sanitization_safety_valve] Pass if the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:103-110 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:297-305 contain at least one concrete destabilizing action (not summary) and no more than one explicit moralizing reassurance line; fail if the passage still tells the reader how to judge the scene instead of showing uncertain aftermath behavior.

[ch08_booker_001_consequence_underload] Pass if lines in the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:251-311 include one irreversible or behavior-constraining action taken on-page (not just a promise) and the final paragraph still carries unresolved risk; fail if the ending remains primarily affective closure plus stated intent.

[ch08_dialogue_001_idiolect_convergence] Pass if, in revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:245-257, each character has at least two lines that clearly map to their style-bible voice signatures and there is no repeated one-word echo pair across adjacent turns; fail if their replies remain rhythmically and lexically interchangeable.

[ch08_prose_001_recurring_negation_formula] Pass if the revised chapter uses the 'no/not X, no/not Y' template no more than twice total in the cited span set and at least two replaced lines are rendered as concrete affirmative action/sensation; fail if the same negation template still recurs at three or more of those pivots.

[ch08_craft_002_timidity_post_peak] Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:283-285 includes one explicit, embodied discomfort beat that neither character resolves immediately; fail if the text still transitions directly from catharsis to banter without an unstable aftermath moment.

[FBK-006] PASS if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2529-2534 no longer uses an 'almost ... instead ...' suppression construction and still clearly conveys hesitation plus commitment. FAIL if the same template remains.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:103-110 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:297-305 contain at least one concrete destabilizing action (not summary) and no more than one explicit moralizing reassurance line; fail if the passage still tells the reader how to judge the scene instead of showing uncertain aftermath behavior.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift appears as repeated moral-safety-valve narration at peak-pressure beats (e.g., anti-halo framing and explicit reassurance language) that pre-interprets the scene for the reader and softens unresolved danger.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:103-110, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:235-236, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:297-305 by replacing interpretive/moral labeling with observable consequence (hesitation, misattunement, bodily recoil, or a concrete trust-risk behavior). Keep one value statement maximum and let the rest of the stakes emerge through action/dialogue.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if lines in the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:251-311 include one irreversible or behavior-constraining action taken on-page (not just a promise) and the final paragraph still carries unresolved risk; fail if the ending remains primarily affective closure plus stated intent.
findings.problem: Causally, the chapter resolves into comfort too cleanly after relapse disclosure and boundary ultimatums. Most consequences are verbal promises, so the promised 'higher-stakes bond' lands with limited immediate cost or constraint.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:251-311, add one immediate, concrete consequence that changes tomorrow's options (e.g., a non-symbolic accountability action, a boundary enforcement mechanism, or a sacrificed convenience) before the closing quiet beat.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:245-257, each character has at least two lines that clearly map to their style-bible voice signatures and there is no repeated one-word echo pair across adjacent turns; fail if their replies remain rhythmically and lexically interchangeable.
findings.problem: Marisol and Ada briefly collapse into interchangeable compliance cadence (echoed one-word replies and shared contract diction), weakening required idiolect separation from the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:245-257 so Ada stays blunt/process-specific while Marisol answers in her image-driven, self-accusing register; remove mirrored lexical echoes (e.g., duplicated single-word confirmations) unless one is intentionally strategic.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter uses the 'no/not X, no/not Y' template no more than twice total in the cited span set and at least two replaced lines are rendered as concrete affirmative action/sensation; fail if the same negation template still recurs at three or more of those pivots.
findings.problem: A recurring 'no/not X, no/not Y' construction appears more than twice and starts reading as a prose tic, flattening cadence at major emotional pivots.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:183, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:297 to vary sentence architecture (affirmative sensory statements, concrete verbs, and asymmetric clause lengths) instead of repeating negation pair templates.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:283-285 includes one explicit, embodied discomfort beat that neither character resolves immediately; fail if the text still transitions directly from catharsis to banter without an unstable aftermath moment.
findings.problem: Timidity: after a high-voltage confession/intimacy peak, the chapter pivots quickly into playful shower banter and soft montage, skipping a beat of messy disorientation that the premise pressure earns.
findings.rewrite_direction: Between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:283-285, insert a short aftermath beat with physical/emotional friction (awkward silence, mismatched regulation, or relapse-trigger residue) before humor resumes.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2529-2534 no longer uses an 'almost ... instead ...' suppression construction and still clearly conveys hesitation plus commitment. FAIL if the same template remains.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern: suppression template ('almost said ... instead said ...') appears in at least 4 chapters (08, 11, 12, 15). In this chapter, the template blunts immediacy at a key boundary/consent beat. Decision for this chapter: REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2529-2534 so Marisol's hesitation is conveyed through concrete action/voice texture without the 'almost said' scaffold. Keep the relational stakes and Ada's boundary pressure unchanged.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information
